prose:
**Fukui reactivity analysis of phenol** — DFT, tier `standard` (**B3LYP/def2-TZVP**, PySCF; density fitting on by default), **gas phase** (no implicit solvent), reference (neutral) state charge 0 / multiplicity 1. The finite-difference Fukui functions were built from three SCF calculations: neutral (charge 0, mult 1), cation (charge +1, mult 2, open-shell UKS), and anion (charge −1, mult 2, open-shell UKS). Partial charges use the Mulliken (PySCF) scheme. Geometry: user-supplied `phenol.xyz` (13 atoms: C6H5OH), used as-is — this was a single-point-based descriptor calculation, not a geometry optimization; the coordinates were not re-relaxed at this level.

**Headline result — most electrophilic site (largest f⁺):** atom index 3 (1-based), a ring **carbon**, with **f⁺ = 2.1028319**. The most nucleophilic site (largest f⁻) is the **oxygen** (atom 1, 1-based), f⁻ = 0.16201477. The dual descriptor (f⁺ − f⁻) is likewise largest at C3 (2.0318595), reinforcing it as the preferred electrophilic-attack center.

Note on interpretation and significant figures: these are semi-quantitative, method- and geometry-dependent descriptors derived from Mulliken charges, which are known to be basis-set sensitive; the several-decimal precision reflects the raw tool output, not physical accuracy. Some individual atomic f⁺/f⁻ values are negative and some magnitudes are large (e.g. the anion's Mulliken charges are quite delocalized), which is typical of Mulliken-based finite-difference Fukui indices and not itself an error.

**Method/backend:** PySCF, DFT RKS (neutral) / UKS (open-shell ions). Command: `.../cli.py fukui --method dft --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp --tier standard .../phenol.xyz`. A plot was written to `.../20260702-225322_phenol_fukui_reactivity/agent_call_01_plot.png`.
